High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall implement quality checking processes as soon as data become available.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall implement quality checking processes as soon as data become available.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall expose a RESTful API endpoint for administrators to enable or disable quality checking for individual data streams."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall maintain a configuration database table that stores quality checking rules, including criteria and thresholds, for each data source."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall listen to the data ingestion event bus and, upon detection of new data, immediately enqueue a quality checking task."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall execute quality checking tasks in a separate worker thread to avoid blocking other administrative operations."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall record the start time, completion status, and any anomalies detected during quality checking in an audit log."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall provide a web-based administrative console that displays real-time metrics of quality checking execution, such as throughput and error rates."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAS shall expose a RESTful API endpoint for administrators to enable or disable quality checking for individual data streams.
The CAS shall maintain a configuration database table that stores quality checking rules, including criteria and thresholds, for each data source.
The CAS shall listen to the data ingestion event bus and, upon detection of new data, immediately enqueue a quality checking task.
The CAS shall execute quality checking tasks in a separate worker thread to avoid blocking other administrative operations.
The CAS shall record the start time, completion status, and any anomalies detected during quality checking in an audit log.
The CAS shall provide a web-based administrative console that displays real-time metrics of quality checking execution, such as throughput and error rates.

The Quality Checking Services shall apply range validation checks to ensure measured values fall within defined physical limits.
The Quality Checking Services shall flag and log any data anomalies for review by the Environmental Metadata Services.
The Quality Checking Services shall generate a quality status report and make it available to the Qualified Environmental Data Services within 200ms.
The Quality Checking Services shall retry failed validation checks up to three times with exponential backoff before marking data as invalid.

The CAS shall associate each recorded method with the corresponding quality checking result and source data identifiers.
The CAS shall provide an administrative interface to query and retrieve recorded method information for audit purposes.
The CAS shall enforce access control to ensure only authorized administrators can modify or delete recorded method entries.
The CAS shall log any modifications to recorded method entries, including the user, timestamp, and change description.

raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CAUI","requirement":"The CAUI shall provide a dedicated configuration screen where users can define quality checking rules for each environmental parameter type (e.g., temperature, humidity, pressure)."},{"allocation":"CAUI","requirement":"The CAUI shall allow users to specify rule conditions, including threshold values, ranges, and logical operators, for each environmental parameter within the quality checking rule editor."},{"allocation":"CAUI","requirement":"The CAUI shall display real-time validation feedback when a user enters or modifies a quality checking rule, highlighting syntax errors or logical inconsistencies."},{"allocation":"CAUI","requirement":"The CAUI shall enable users to assign severity levels and associated alert actions to each quality checking rule for environmental parameters."},{"allocation":"CAUI","requirement":"The CAUI shall persist all configured quality checking rules in a central repository and synchronize them with the Quality Checking Services (QChS) upon user-initiated save."},{"allocation":"CAUI","requirement":"The CAUI shall present a searchable and filterable list of existing quality checking rules, grouped by environmental parameter, with options to edit or delete each rule."},{"allocation":"CAUI","requirement":"The CAUI shall enforce role-based access control such that only authorized administrators can create, modify, or delete quality checking rules for environmental parameters."}]}

The CAUI shall allow users to specify rule conditions, including threshold values, ranges, and logical operators, for each environmental parameter within the quality checking rule editor.
The CAUI shall display real-time validation feedback when a user enters or modifies a quality checking rule, highlighting syntax errors or logical inconsistencies.
The CAUI shall enable users to assign severity levels and associated alert actions to each quality checking rule for environmental parameters.
The CAUI shall persist all configured quality checking rules in a central repository and synchronize them with the Quality Checking Services (QChS) upon user-initiated save.
The CAUI shall present a searchable and filterable list of existing quality checking rules, grouped by environmental parameter, with options to edit or delete each rule.
The CAUI shall enforce role-based access control such that only authorized administrators can create, modify, or delete quality checking rules for environmental parameters.

raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall provide a configuration interface that allows administrators to select one or more historical environmental data sources for use in quality checking processes."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall store configuration data specifying the retention period for historical environmental data used in quality checking, with a default retention period of at least five years."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall expose an API endpoint /config/quality/historical that returns a JSON representation of the current historical data configuration, including source identifiers and retention settings."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall validate that any historical data source identifier specified in the configuration exists in the system's data source registry."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall persist all configuration changes related to historical environmental data to a durable storage medium and ensure they are applied to the quality checking process after a system restart."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall maintain an audit log of all modifications to historical data configuration, recording the user, timestamp, and change details."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall enforce role-based access control such that only users assigned the Administrator role may modify the historical environmental data configuration for quality checking."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAS shall provide a configuration interface that allows administrators to select one or more historical environmental data sources for use in quality checking processes.
The CAS shall store configuration data specifying the retention period for historical environmental data used in quality checking, with a default retention period of at least five years.
The CAS shall expose an API endpoint /config/quality/historical that returns a JSON representation of the current historical data configuration, including source identifiers and retention settings.
The CAS shall validate that any historical data source identifier specified in the configuration exists in the system's data source registry.

The CAS shall persist all configuration changes related to historical environmental data to a durable storage medium and ensure they are applied to the quality checking process after a system restart.
The CAS shall maintain an audit log of all modifications to historical data configuration, recording the user, timestamp, and change details.
The CAS shall enforce role-based access control such that only users assigned the Administrator role may modify the historical environmental data configuration for quality checking.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAS shall provide a configuration interface that allows administrators to select one or more historical environmental data sources for use in quality checking processes.

The CAS shall enforce role-based access control such that only users assigned the Administrator role may modify the historical environmental data configuration for quality checking.

The Quality Checking Services shall verify that each data record contains all mandatory fields, including timestamp, geographic location, and pollutant identifier, and reject records missing any mandatory field.
The Quality Checking Services shall detect and reject duplicate records by comparing unique record identifiers and timestamps, ensuring each data submission is unique.
The Quality Checking Services shall perform range and plausibility checks on quantitative environmental measurements to ensure values fall within scientifically acceptable limits.

The Quality Checking Services shall generate a daily quality summary report that includes the total number of records processed, accepted, rejected, and pending review, broken down by data source.

The CAS shall enforce role-based access control to allow only administrators to perform security group management operations.
The CAS shall log all security group management actions, including timestamp, user identifier, and operation type, for audit purposes.
The CAS shall persist security group modifications in durable storage with transactional integrity.
The CAS shall provide appropriate error information for failed security group operations.

The Configuration & Administration Service shall generate an audit log entry for any publication request that is rejected due to an unauthorized source.
The Configuration & Administration Service shall expose an administrative API that allows adding, updating, and deleting source restriction rules without requiring system restart.
The Configuration & Administration Service shall validate source restriction rules for syntax and consistency upon any configuration change and reject invalid configurations.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAS shall provide a REST API endpoint at /api/v1/admin/statistics that accepts HTTP POST requests containing operational statistics payloads from other system components.
The CAS shall validate each incoming statistics payload against a defined JSON schema to ensure required fields and data types are present before persisting.
The CAS shall persist all validated operational statistics in a persistent relational database table with a configurable retention period expressed in days.

The CAS shall expose a query interface via the Configuration & Administration User Interface that allows administrators to retrieve statistics records filtered by time range, subsystem, and statistic type.
The CAS shall generate daily aggregate reports of recorded statistics and make them available for download in CSV format through the administrative web interface.
The CAS shall enforce role-based access control such that only users with the Administrator role can view, query, or delete recorded statistical data.

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall log data transactions.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall capture and persistently store each data transaction in a log entry that includes a timestamp, source identifier, transaction type, and transaction payload."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall generate a unique identifier for each log entry to ensure traceability."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall store log entries in a tamper‑evident format (e.g., cryptographic hash chain) to prevent unauthorized modifications."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall provide an API for authorized components to retrieve log entries based on criteria such as time range, source, or transaction type."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall enforce a minimum retention period of seven years for all log entries, after which entries may be archived or purged per policy."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall retry failed log writes up to three attempts with exponential back‑off before raising an alert to the monitoring system."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall log all data transactions regardless of whether the transaction succeeded or failed, including error details for failures."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall format each log entry as a JSON object conforming to the Collector Services Log Schema defined in the system documentation."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall ensure that logging operations complete within 100 milliseconds under normal load conditions on a single‑core processor."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall support concurrent logging of at least 1000 transactions per second without loss of entries."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall capture and persistently store each data transaction in a log entry that includes a timestamp, source identifier, transaction type, and transaction payload.
CS shall generate a unique identifier for each log entry to ensure traceability.
CS shall store log entries in a tamper‑evident format (e.g., cryptographic hash chain) to prevent unauthorized modifications.
CS shall provide an API for authorized components to retrieve log entries based on criteria such as time range, source, or transaction type.
CS shall enforce a minimum retention period of seven years for all log entries, after which entries may be archived or purged per policy.
CS shall retry failed log writes up to three attempts with exponential back‑off before raising an alert to the monitoring system.
CS shall log all data transactions regardless of whether the transaction succeeded or failed, including error details for failures.
CS shall format each log entry as a JSON object conforming to the Collector Services Log Schema defined in the system documentation.
CS shall ensure that logging operations complete within 100 milliseconds under normal load conditions on a single‑core processor.
CS shall support concurrent logging of at least 1000 transactions per second without loss of entries.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall manage environmental data and metadata according to the Clarus data sharing agreements.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall manage environmental data and metadata according to the Clarus data sharing agreements.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall provide a REST API for creating, reading, updating, and deleting configuration parameters required for environmental data management."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall enforce access control policies that restrict configuration changes to authorized administrators based on role-based permissions."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall maintain an audit log of all configuration actions, including user identity, timestamp, and change details, for compliance with data sharing agreements."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall allow administrators to define data sharing agreement rules that specify which environmental data sets may be shared with external parties and under what conditions."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall validate all configuration changes against a defined schema to ensure consistency and prevent invalid settings that could affect environmental data handling."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall provide a user interface for administrators to view and modify configuration settings, supporting multi-language localization."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall support backup and restore of configuration data to ensure recoverability after system failures."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"The CAS shall synchronize configuration changes across all instances of the Clarus system in a distributed environment within a maximum latency of 5 seconds."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAS shall provide a REST API for creating, reading, updating, and deleting configuration parameters required for environmental data management.
The CAS shall enforce access control policies that restrict configuration changes to authorized administrators based on role-based permissions.
The CAS shall maintain an audit log of all configuration actions, including user identity, timestamp, and change details, for compliance with data sharing agreements.
The CAS shall allow administrators to define data sharing agreement rules that specify which environmental data sets may be shared with external parties and under what conditions.
The CAS shall validate all configuration changes against a defined schema to ensure consistency and prevent invalid settings that could affect environmental data handling.
The CAS shall provide a user interface for administrators to view and modify configuration settings, supporting multi-language localization.
The CAS shall support backup and restore of configuration data to ensure recoverability after system failures.
The CAS shall synchronize configuration changes across all instances of the Clarus system in a distributed environment within a maximum latency of 5 seconds.

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAUI shall present a configurable view of all active environmental data sharing agreements, including partner identifiers, data types, and validity periods.
CAUI shall enable authorized users to create new sharing agreements by entering required fields and uploading supporting documents.
CAUI shall provide inline validation for sharing agreement fields, rejecting entries that do not conform to the Clarus agreement schema.
CAUI shall display real-time status indicators for each data source, showing whether the latest environmental data has been successfully shared according to the agreement.
CAUI shall allow administrators to modify existing sharing agreements, with change tracking and version control.
CAUI shall enforce role‑based access control, ensuring that only users with Configuration & Administration privileges can edit sharing agreements.
CAUI shall log every configuration change (create, update, delete) with timestamp, user ID, and previous and new values for audit purposes.
CAUI shall generate and export a PDF report of all sharing agreements and their current compliance status on request.
CAUI shall issue alerts and notifications when a sharing agreement is about to expire or when data sharing violations are detected.
CAUI shall be accessible via a web browser and shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards.

The CAUI shall enable configuration of data source endpoints, including network addresses, authentication tokens, and sampling intervals for the Collector Services.

The CAUI shall support import and export of system configuration files in JSON format to facilitate backup and migration.
The CAUI shall display system alerts and logs, permitting administrators to acknowledge incidents and view detailed event histories.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall accept incoming environmental data from all supported sensor types using the defined communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP POST, serial)."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall validate the structure and completeness of each incoming data packet against the defined schema before storing it."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall assign a UTC timestamp to each collected data point and store it alongside the raw values."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall provide a configurable buffer to retain collected data during network interruptions and forward it when connectivity is restored."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall expose collected data to downstream subsystems through a RESTful API that returns data in JSON format conforming to the system data model."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall allow administrators to configure sampling intervals and data retention policies per sensor source."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall log all collection events, including source identifier, data size, and result status, for audit and troubleshooting purposes."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall perform deduplication of incoming data based on source ID and timestamp to prevent duplicate storage."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall accept incoming environmental data from all supported sensor types using the defined communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP POST, serial).
CS shall validate the structure and completeness of each incoming data packet against the defined schema before storing it.
CS shall assign a UTC timestamp to each collected data point and store it alongside the raw values.
CS shall provide a configurable buffer to retain collected data during network interruptions and forward it when connectivity is restored.
CS shall expose collected data to downstream subsystems through a RESTful API that returns data in JSON format conforming to the system data model.
CS shall allow administrators to configure sampling intervals and data retention policies per sensor source.
CS shall log all collection events, including source identifier, data size, and result status, for audit and troubleshooting purposes.
CS shall perform deduplication of incoming data based on source ID and timestamp to prevent duplicate storage.

The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"QEDS","requirement":"The QEDS shall ingest raw environmental data from Collector Services via a secure REST API within 5 seconds of receipt."},{"allocation":"QEDS","requirement":"The QEDS shall validate incoming data against defined schema and reject any record that fails validation, logging the error with timestamp and source."},{"allocation":"QEDS","requirement":"The QEDS shall compute derived metrics such as hourly averages and daily maxima for each sensor type and store them in the Qualified Environmental Data cache."},{"allocation":"QEDS","requirement":"The QEDS shall provide a query interface that returns qualified data in JSON format, supporting filters by time range, location, and sensor type."},{"allocation":"QEDS","requirement":"The QEDS shall expose a publish/subscribe endpoint for real-time data dissemination to downstream services, guaranteeing at-least-once delivery."},{"allocation":"QEDS","requirement":"The QEDS shall maintain an audit log of all data transformations, including original values, transformed values, and transformation rules applied."},{"allocation":"QEDS","requirement":"The QEDS shall perform automated health checks and report status to the Watchdog service every 30 seconds, indicating data ingestion rate and processing latency."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QEDS shall ingest raw environmental data from Collector Services via a secure REST API within 5 seconds of receipt.
The QEDS shall validate incoming data against defined schema and reject any record that fails validation, logging the error with timestamp and source.
The QEDS shall compute derived metrics such as hourly averages and daily maxima for each sensor type and store them in the Qualified Environmental Data cache.
The QEDS shall provide a query interface that returns qualified data in JSON format, supporting filters by time range, location, and sensor type.
The QEDS shall expose a publish/subscribe endpoint for real-time data dissemination to downstream services, guaranteeing at-least-once delivery.
The QEDS shall maintain an audit log of all data transformations, including original values, transformed values, and transformation rules applied.
The QEDS shall perform automated health checks and report status to the Watchdog service every 30 seconds, indicating data ingestion rate and processing latency.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QEDS shall ingest raw environmental data from Collector Services within 5 seconds of receipt by the QEDS.
The QEDS shall validate incoming data against a defined schema and reject any record that fails validation.
The QEDS shall log each validation failure with a timestamp and the source of the data.
The QEDS shall compute derived metrics, including hourly averages and daily maxima, for each sensor type.
The QEDS shall store computed derived metrics in the Qualified Environmental Data store.
The QEDS shall provide a query interface that returns qualified environmental data in JSON format, supporting filters by time range, location, and sensor type.
The QEDS shall provide a real-time data dissemination capability to downstream services, guaranteeing at-least-once delivery.
The QEDS shall maintain an audit log of all data transformations, including original values, transformed values, and transformation rules applied.
The QEDS shall perform automated health checks every 30 seconds, measuring data ingestion rate and processing latency.
The QEDS shall report health check results to the Watchdog service every 30 seconds.

The component shall apply defined validation rules to assign a default quality flag when none is provided by the source data.
The component shall expose a read‑only API that returns environmental data records including their current quality flags.
The component shall ensure quality flags are immutable after persistence, except when updated through an authorized correction workflow.
The component shall log any change to a quality flag with a timestamp, user identifier, and reason for the change.
The component shall support retrieval of environmental data filtered by quality flag criteria (e.g., only records with flag "GOOD").

raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall provide a web-based user interface for human operators to review and validate environmental data entries."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall allow operators to assign a quality status of Approved, Rejected, or Flagged to each data record."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall record the operator identifier and timestamp for every quality check action performed."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall maintain an immutable audit trail of all quality check decisions and comments."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring only authorized personnel can perform quality checks."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall present visual indicators (e.g., color-coded badges) reflecting the current quality status of each data record."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall require completion of mandatory validation checks before allowing submission of a quality decision."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall allow operators to attach free-form comments or notes to explain quality decisions."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall prevent modification of approved data unless a new quality check is initiated."},{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"The Quality Checking Services shall generate a summary report of quality check statistics accessible to system administrators."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall provide a web-based user interface for human operators to review and validate environmental data entries.
The Quality Checking Services shall allow operators to assign a quality status of Approved, Rejected, or Flagged to each data record.
The Quality Checking Services shall record the operator identifier and timestamp for every quality check action performed.
The Quality Checking Services shall maintain an immutable audit trail of all quality check decisions and comments.
The Quality Checking Services shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring only authorized personnel can perform quality checks.
The Quality Checking Services shall present visual indicators (e.g., color-coded badges) reflecting the current quality status of each data record.
The Quality Checking Services shall require completion of mandatory validation checks before allowing submission of a quality decision.
The Quality Checking Services shall allow operators to attach free-form comments or notes to explain quality decisions.
The Quality Checking Services shall prevent modification of approved data unless a new quality check is initiated.
The Quality Checking Services shall generate a summary report of quality check statistics accessible to system administrators.

raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"Collector Services shall establish and maintain a persistent TCP/IP connection to each environmental sensor station collector using the defined communication protocol (e.g., MQTT, HTTP) to enable real-time data retrieval."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"Collector Services shall request and receive raw environmental data packets from sensor stations at a minimum frequency of every 5 seconds."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"Collector Services shall parse incoming data packets according to the defined schema and extract fields including timestamp, sensor ID, temperature, humidity, pressure, and quality flags."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"Collector Services shall validate the integrity of each received data packet using a checksum or CRC and reject any packet that fails validation."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"Collector Services shall store retrieved environmental data in a local buffer for a minimum retention period of 24 hours to support retransmission or reprocessing."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"Collector Services shall implement connection failure handling with retry logic using exponential backoff and shall log all connection events and errors."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"Collector Services shall forward validated environmental data to the Qualified Environmental Data Services (QEDS) via the defined API within 100 milliseconds of receipt."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: Collector Services shall establish and maintain a persistent TCP/IP connection to each environmental sensor station collector using the defined communication protocol (e.g., MQTT, HTTP) to enable real-time data retrieval.
Collector Services shall request and receive raw environmental data packets from sensor stations at a minimum frequency of every 5 seconds.
Collector Services shall parse incoming data packets according to the defined schema and extract fields including timestamp, sensor ID, temperature, humidity, pressure, and quality flags.

Collector Services shall forward validated environmental data to the Qualified Environmental Data Services (QEDS) via the defined API within 100 milliseconds of receipt.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall listen on TCP port 8080 for incoming data packets and acknowledge receipt with an ACK message within 50 milliseconds.

The Collector Services shall be capable of processing at least 1,000 concurrent data packets per second with less than 1% packet loss.

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from railway systems or in-situ ESS along tracks.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall expose a REST API endpoint at /api/v1/collect for receiving environmental data via HTTP POST requests."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall accept data formatted as JSON conforming to the Clarus environmental data schema version 1.2."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall validate that each data record contains mandatory fields: timestamp (UTC), sourceId, location (latitude/longitude), and sensor readings."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall reject any data record missing mandatory fields and return HTTP 400 with a detailed error message."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall assign a UTC timestamp to each received record if not provided, using the system's clock synchronized via NTP."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall store each accepted record in a persistent database with a retention period of at least 30 days."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall forward validated records to the Environmental Metadata Services (EMS) via a synchronous HTTP POST within 5 seconds of receipt."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall implement duplicate detection by comparing sourceId and timestamp within a configurable window (default 10 seconds) and ignore duplicates."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall log each data receipt event, including sourceId, timestamp, size, and outcome, to a secure audit log."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall provide a health-check endpoint at /health that returns HTTP 200 with status \"OK\" when the service is operational."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall support TLS 1.2 encryption for all inbound and outbound communications."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall enforce authentication of data sources using API keys validated against the Configuration & Administration Service (CAS)."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall expose a REST API endpoint at /api/v1/collect for receiving environmental data via HTTP POST requests.
CS shall accept data formatted as JSON conforming to the Clarus environmental data schema version 1.2.
CS shall validate that each data record contains mandatory fields: timestamp (UTC), sourceId, location (latitude/longitude), and sensor readings.
CS shall reject any data record missing mandatory fields and return HTTP 400 with a detailed error message.
CS shall assign a UTC timestamp to each received record if not provided, using the system's clock synchronized via NTP.
CS shall store each accepted record in a persistent database with a retention period of at least 30 days.
CS shall forward validated records to the Environmental Metadata Services (EMS) via a synchronous HTTP POST within 5 seconds of receipt.
CS shall implement duplicate detection by comparing sourceId and timestamp within a configurable window (default 10 seconds) and ignore duplicates.
CS shall log each data receipt event, including sourceId, timestamp, size, and outcome, to a secure audit log.
CS shall provide a health-check endpoint at /health that returns HTTP 200 with status "OK" when the service is operational.
CS shall support TLS 1.2 encryption for all inbound and outbound communications.
CS shall enforce authentication of data sources using API keys validated against the Configuration & Administration Service (CAS).

Collector Services shall validate incoming environmental data against the required schema (temperature, humidity, pressure, location) and reject any invalid data with an appropriate error response.
Collector Services shall timestamp each received data packet with UTC and persist it in a database for a minimum retention period of 90 days.
Collector Services shall forward validated environmental data to Qualified Environmental Data Services (QEDS) within a maximum latency of 500 milliseconds.
Collector Services shall detect and ignore duplicate data packets based on vehicle identifier and timestamp to prevent redundant processing.
Collector Services shall expose a health monitoring endpoint accessible by the Watchdog (DOG) to report operational status and any detected failures.
Collector Services shall generate and retain audit logs of all data reception events and processing errors for a minimum of 180 days for compliance and troubleshooting purposes.

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from maintenance and construction vehicles.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall accept environmental data from maintenance and construction vehicles via a defined communication protocol (e.g., MQTT) and acknowledge receipt within 2 seconds."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall validate incoming environmental data against a predefined schema, rejecting any data that does not conform and logging validation failures."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall persist validated environmental data in a relational database with a timestamp, vehicle identifier, and data payload."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall provide a RESTful API allowing downstream services to query environmental data filtered by vehicle ID, date range, and data type."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall handle duplicate data entries by ignoring records with identical vehicle ID and timestamp within a 5‑minute window."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall support concurrent data ingestion from at least 100 vehicles with a maximum latency of 500 ms per message."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall queue incoming data locally during network outages and automatically re‑transmit once connectivity is restored."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall generate an alert to the monitoring system when an environmental data value exceeds defined thresholds."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall accept environmental data from maintenance and construction vehicles via a defined communication protocol (e.g., MQTT) and acknowledge receipt within 2 seconds.
CS shall validate incoming environmental data against a predefined schema, rejecting any data that does not conform and logging validation failures.
CS shall persist validated environmental data in a relational database with a timestamp, vehicle identifier, and data payload.
CS shall provide a RESTful API allowing downstream services to query environmental data filtered by vehicle ID, date range, and data type.
CS shall handle duplicate data entries by ignoring records with identical vehicle ID and timestamp within a 5‑minute window.
CS shall support concurrent data ingestion from at least 100 vehicles with a maximum latency of 500 ms per message.
CS shall queue incoming data locally during network outages and automatically re‑transmit once connectivity is restored.
CS shall generate an alert to the monitoring system when an environmental data value exceeds defined thresholds.

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall receive environmental data messages via UDP port 5000 from transit vehicles.
CS shall parse each received message according to the Clarus environmental data schema version 1.2.
CS shall validate that each data point includes required fields: vehicle ID, timestamp, temperature, humidity, and GPS coordinates.
CS shall store validated environmental data in the Collector Database with a retention period of 365 days.
CS shall forward accepted environmental data to the Environmental Metadata Services (EMS) within 100 milliseconds of receipt.
CS shall generate an acknowledgment message for each successfully processed data submission.
CS shall log all data reception events, including source IP, timestamp, and processing outcome, for audit purposes.
CS shall handle duplicate data submissions by ignoring subsequent identical messages within a 5-second window.
CS shall operate with a minimum availability of 99.5% over a calendar month.

Target subsystem: Collector Services

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from emergency vehicles.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall expose a RESTful API endpoint at /collector/api/v1/environmental that accepts HTTP POST requests containing environmental data from emergency vehicles."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall validate each incoming environmental data payload to ensure it conforms to the defined schema, including required fields such as vehicleId, timestamp, temperature, and humidity."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall reject any environmental data payload that fails validation and return an HTTP 400 response with detailed error information."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall persist accepted environmental data in the Collector Database within 500 milliseconds of receipt."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall generate a unique acknowledgment message for each successfully accepted environmental data payload and send it to the originating vehicle."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall log every data acceptance event, including vehicle identifier, timestamp, and payload hash, to the audit log for traceability."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall implement retry logic to reattempt data transmission from emergency vehicles in case of network failures, with a maximum of three retries and exponential backoff."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"CS shall enforce authentication of emergency vehicles using a valid API token before processing environmental data."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall expose a RESTful API endpoint at /collector/api/v1/environmental that accepts HTTP POST requests containing environmental data from emergency vehicles.
CS shall validate each incoming environmental data payload to ensure it conforms to the defined schema, including required fields such as vehicleId, timestamp, temperature, and humidity.
CS shall reject any environmental data payload that fails validation and return an HTTP 400 response with detailed error information.
CS shall persist accepted environmental data in the Collector Database within 500 milliseconds of receipt.
CS shall generate a unique acknowledgment message for each successfully accepted environmental data payload and send it to the originating vehicle.
CS shall log every data acceptance event, including vehicle identifier, timestamp, and payload hash, to the audit log for traceability.
CS shall implement retry logic to reattempt data transmission from emergency vehicles in case of network failures, with a maximum of three retries and exponential backoff.
CS shall enforce authentication of emergency vehicles using a valid API token before processing environmental data.

low_level_requirements.requirement: CS shall provide an interface for receiving environmental data from emergency vehicles via HTTP POST requests.
CS shall validate each incoming environmental data payload against a defined schema that includes the required fields vehicleId, timestamp, temperature, and humidity.
CS shall reject any environmental data payload that fails validation and provide error information.
CS shall persist accepted environmental data in its database within 500 milliseconds of receipt.
CS shall generate a unique acknowledgment for each successfully accepted environmental data payload and send it to the originating vehicle.
CS shall log each data acceptance event, including vehicle identifier, timestamp, and payload hash, to an audit log for traceability.
CS shall authenticate emergency vehicles using a valid API token before processing environmental data.

The Clarus system shall be able to receive weather data from weather service providers.

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall be able to receive weather data from weather service providers.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall implement a RESTful API endpoint to accept HTTP POST requests containing weather data payloads from weather service providers."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall authenticate weather service providers using API keys or OAuth 2.0 tokens before processing incoming data."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall parse and validate incoming weather data according to a defined schema, ensuring required fields such as temperature, humidity, wind speed, and timestamp are present and correctly formatted."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall persist received weather data in a durable data store with indexing on timestamp and provider identifier for efficient retrieval."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall log each data reception event, including source, timestamp, and processing outcome, for audit and troubleshooting purposes."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall handle transient network failures by retrying failed data transmissions up to three times with exponential backoff intervals."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall expose a health-check endpoint that returns operational status and indicates any ongoing issues with data reception."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall implement a RESTful API endpoint to accept HTTP POST requests containing weather data payloads from weather service providers.
The Collector Services shall authenticate weather service providers using API keys or OAuth 2.0 tokens before processing incoming data.

The Collector Services shall parse and validate incoming weather data according to a defined schema, ensuring required fields such as temperature, humidity, wind speed, and timestamp are present and correctly formatted.
The Collector Services shall persist received weather data in a durable data store with indexing on timestamp and provider identifier for efficient retrieval.
The Collector Services shall log each data reception event, including source, timestamp, and processing outcome, for audit and troubleshooting purposes.
The Collector Services shall handle transient network failures by retrying failed data transmissions up to three times with exponential backoff intervals.
The Collector Services shall expose a health-check endpoint that returns operational status and indicates any ongoing issues with data reception.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall validate that each observation contains a location field with both latitude and longitude values expressed as decimal numbers with a minimum precision of four decimal places.

The Collector Services shall validate that each observation contains a timestamp field formatted according to ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) and representing a time not more than 24 hours in the future or past relative to the system clock.

The Collector Services shall validate that each observation contains a source metadata field that includes at least a source identifier (UUID or alphanumeric), source type (e.g., sensor, manual entry), and acquisition method (e.g., direct, API, file upload).

If any required field (location, timestamp, source metadata) is missing, null, or invalid, the Collector Services shall reject the observation and respond with HTTP status code 400 and a JSON error payload specifying the missing or invalid field(s).

The Collector Services shall log every rejected observation attempt, capturing the observation identifier (if present), the validation failure reason, the timestamp of the attempt, and the source IP address.

The Collector Services shall enforce geographic bounds by rejecting observations whose location coordinates fall outside the defined operational region, returning a 400 response with an error indicating the geographic violation.

The Collector Services shall enforce a configurable maximum observation latency (default 30 minutes) such that observations with timestamps exceeding this latency relative to the current system time are rejected with a 400 response indicating the latency violation.

Upon successful validation, the Collector Services shall persist the observation in the primary data store, storing the original location, timestamp, and source metadata fields unchanged, and assign a unique observation identifier.

The Collector Services shall support concurrent data ingestion from at least 100 simultaneous connections using the Clarus standard protocols.
The Collector Services shall enforce a configurable timeout of no more than 5 seconds for completing the data acceptance process.
The Collector Services shall store accepted data in a persistent buffer capable of retaining data for a minimum of 30 days without loss.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to communicate with RWIS databases through their native interfaces.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall be able to communicate with RWIS databases through their native interfaces.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall implement a TCP/IP client module capable of establishing a persistent connection to RWIS database servers using the native RWIS TCP port 5432."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall parse RWIS database query responses according to the native RWIS protocol specification, including handling of timestamped sensor data fields."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall support configurable retry logic with an exponential backoff algorithm to recover from transient communication failures with RWIS databases."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall log all communication events with RWIS databases, including connection attempts, successful data retrieval, and error codes, in a structured log format."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall provide a health-check endpoint that reports the status of the RWIS database connections, including latency and last successful synchronization timestamp."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall be capable of concurrently handling up to 50 simultaneous RWIS database connections without exceeding 80% CPU utilization on the host server."},{"allocation":"CS","requirement":"The Collector Services shall validate the integrity of retrieved RWIS data using checksums embedded in the native protocol before forwarding the data to upstream processing components."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall implement a TCP/IP client module capable of establishing a persistent connection to RWIS database servers using the native RWIS TCP port 5432.
The Collector Services shall parse RWIS database query responses according to the native RWIS protocol specification, including handling of timestamped sensor data fields.
The Collector Services shall support configurable retry logic with an exponential backoff algorithm to recover from transient communication failures with RWIS databases.
The Collector Services shall log all communication events with RWIS databases, including connection attempts, successful data retrieval, and error codes, in a structured log format.
The Collector Services shall provide a health-check endpoint that reports the status of the RWIS database connections, including latency and last successful synchronization timestamp.
The Collector Services shall be capable of concurrently handling up to 50 simultaneous RWIS database connections without exceeding 80% CPU utilization on the host server.
The Collector Services shall validate the integrity of retrieved RWIS data using checksums embedded in the native protocol before forwarding the data to upstream processing components.

ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148-inspired requirement quality criteria:

- Implementation-independent: each LLR avoids unnecessary design choices.

Revision rules:

- Remove LLRs belonging to another subsystem.

- Remove speculative, duplicate, unverifiable, ambiguous, or implementation-specific LLRs.
- Every returned allocation value must be exactly "EMS".
- Preserve the HLR's intent and modal wording.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The EMC shall store metadata using the ISO 19115 standard for geographic information to ensure interoperability with external systems.

The EMC shall implement versioning of metadata records using Semantic Versioning (SemVer) to minimize disruption during updates.
The EMC shall expose metadata through the STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) specification to align with industry best practices for environmental data.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to automatically recover from an unexpected shutdown.

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall be able to automatically recover from an unexpected shutdown.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"DOG","requirement":"The Watchdog shall continuously monitor the operational status of all critical Clarus services and detect any unexpected shutdown within 5 seconds."},{"allocation":"DOG","requirement":"Upon detection of an unexpected shutdown, the Watchdog shall automatically initiate a restart of the affected services and verify successful service initialization before resuming normal operation."},{"allocation":"DOG","requirement":"The Watchdog shall log detailed information about each unexpected shutdown event, including timestamp, affected services, and recovery actions taken, in a persistent log file."},{"allocation":"DOG","requirement":"The Watchdog shall ensure that after recovery, the system restores all active user sessions to their last known state within 30 seconds of service restoration."},{"allocation":"DOG","requirement":"The Watchdog shall provide a health status API that reports the current recovery state and any recent shutdown/recovery events to authorized monitoring tools."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Watchdog shall continuously monitor the operational status of all critical Clarus services and detect any unexpected shutdown within 5 seconds.
Upon detection of an unexpected shutdown, the Watchdog shall automatically initiate a restart of the affected services and verify successful service initialization before resuming normal operation.
The Watchdog shall log detailed information about each unexpected shutdown event, including timestamp, affected services, and recovery actions taken, in a persistent log file.
The Watchdog shall ensure that after recovery, the system restores all active user sessions to their last known state within 30 seconds of service restoration.
The Watchdog shall provide a health status API that reports the current recovery state and any recent shutdown/recovery events to authorized monitoring tools.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall disseminate environmental metadata in response to polling.

Target allocation code: EMS
Target subsystem: Environmental Metadata Services

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall disseminate environmental metadata in response to polling.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall accept polling requests via a RESTful API endpoint /poll with a maximum latency of 100 milliseconds."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall retrieve the most recent environmental metadata from the EMC cache upon receipt of a polling request."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall format the retrieved metadata into JSON and include a UTC timestamp indicating the time of data capture."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall validate the integrity of the metadata using a checksum before responding to the polling request."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall respond to a successful polling request with HTTP status code 200 and the metadata payload."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall respond to a polling request with no available metadata with HTTP status code 404 and an appropriate error message."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall log each polling request, the source client, and the response for audit purposes."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall enforce authentication and authorization checks to ensure only authorized clients can issue polling requests."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall cache the latest metadata to reduce retrieval latency to less than 10 milliseconds for subsequent polling requests."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The EMS shall accept polling requests via a RESTful API endpoint /poll with a maximum latency of 100 milliseconds.
The EMS shall retrieve the most recent environmental metadata from the EMC cache upon receipt of a polling request.
The EMS shall format the retrieved metadata into JSON and include a UTC timestamp indicating the time of data capture.
The EMS shall validate the integrity of the metadata using a checksum before responding to the polling request.
The EMS shall respond to a successful polling request with HTTP status code 200 and the metadata payload.
The EMS shall respond to a polling request with no available metadata with HTTP status code 404 and an appropriate error message.
The EMS shall log each polling request, the source client, and the response for audit purposes.
The EMS shall enforce authentication and authorization checks to ensure only authorized clients can issue polling requests.
The EMS shall cache the latest metadata to reduce retrieval latency to less than 10 milliseconds for subsequent polling requests.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to provide sensor equipment metadata in response to a request.

Target allocation code: EMS
Target subsystem: Environmental Metadata Services

Draft JSON:

high_level_requirement: The Clarus system shall be able to provide sensor equipment metadata in response to a request.
raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall expose a REST API endpoint /sensor/metadata that accepts a GET request with optional query parameters sensorId and sensorType and returns a JSON object containing sensor equipment metadata fields including manufacturer, model, serialNumber, installationDate, location, and calibrationStatus."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall validate request parameters and return a 400 Bad Request response if any provided sensorId is not a valid UUID or if sensorType is not one of the allowed enumerated values."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall retrieve sensor equipment metadata from the sensor registry database and include the latest calibration date and operational status in the response."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall return the sensor metadata response within 500 milliseconds under normal load conditions."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall cache sensor metadata for 5 minutes to improve response performance and consistency."},{"allocation":"EMS","requirement":"The EMS shall log each request for sensor metadata including a unique request identifier, timestamp, and requested sensor identifiers for audit and troubleshooting purposes."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The EMS shall expose a REST API endpoint /sensor/metadata that accepts a GET request with optional query parameters sensorId and sensorType and returns a JSON object containing sensor equipment metadata fields including manufacturer, model, serialNumber, installationDate, location, and calibrationStatus.
The EMS shall validate request parameters and return a 400 Bad Request response if any provided sensorId is not a valid UUID or if sensorType is not one of the allowed enumerated values.
The EMS shall retrieve sensor equipment metadata from the sensor registry database and include the latest calibration date and operational status in the response.
The EMS shall return the sensor metadata response within 500 milliseconds under normal load conditions.
The EMS shall cache sensor metadata for 5 minutes to improve response performance and consistency.
The EMS shall log each request for sensor metadata including a unique request identifier, timestamp, and requested sensor identifiers for audit and troubleshooting purposes.

raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"QEDC","requirement":"The QEDC subsystem shall store incoming environmental data entries in a dynamic library with a retention period of at least seven days from the time of ingestion."},{"allocation":"QEDC","requirement":"The QEDC subsystem shall provide read-only access to the dynamic library for authorized clients, ensuring data is available for the full seven‑day retention period."},{"allocation":"QEDC","requirement":"The QEDC subsystem shall enforce data integrity by applying validation rules to each entry before insertion into the dynamic library."},{"allocation":"QEDC","requirement":"The QEDC subsystem shall support real-time insertion and retrieval of environmental data entries within the dynamic library without degradation of performance."},{"allocation":"QEDC","requirement":"The QEDC subsystem shall generate audit logs for all insert, update, and delete operations performed on the dynamic library entries."},{"allocation":"QEDC","requirement":"The QEDC subsystem shall automatically purge data from the dynamic library after exactly seven days from ingestion unless a retention override is applied."},{"allocation":"QEDC","requirement":"The QEDC subsystem shall provide an interface to query the dynamic library by time range, sensor identifier, and data quality flags."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QEDC subsystem shall store incoming environmental data entries in a dynamic library with a retention period of at least seven days from the time of ingestion.
The QEDC subsystem shall provide read-only access to the dynamic library for authorized clients, ensuring data is available for the full seven‑day retention period.
The QEDC subsystem shall enforce data integrity by applying validation rules to each entry before insertion into the dynamic library.
The QEDC subsystem shall support real-time insertion and retrieval of environmental data entries within the dynamic library without degradation of performance.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QEDS shall expose a RESTful API endpoint /providers/{providerId}/datasets that accepts query parameters including dataType, geographicBounds, and temporalRange to retrieve a list of qualified environmental data sets matching the provider's selection criteria.
The QEDS shall validate that requested data sets are qualified according to QEDC metadata and have passed quality checks performed by QChS before returning them to the service provider.

The QEDS shall allow service providers to confirm their selection of desired data sets by submitting a selection payload containing dataset identifiers, and shall persist the selection in a provider-specific configuration store.
The QEDS shall provide a real-time response indicating success or failure of the selection, including error codes and descriptive messages for any invalid or unavailable data sets.
The QEDS shall maintain an audit log of all selection operations performed by service providers, recording provider identifier, selected dataset identifiers, timestamp, and outcome.
The QEDS shall enforce access control to ensure that a service provider can only select data sets for which they have authorized permissions based on provider role and subscription tier.

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall expose a RESTful API endpoint at /api/v1/data that accepts query parameters startDate, endDate, location, and dataType, and returns a JSON array of qualified environmental data records meeting the criteria.

QEDS shall validate that each returned data record contains mandatory fields: timestamp (ISO 8601), latitude, longitude, dataValue, and dataQualityScore, and shall reject any record missing these fields.
QEDS shall authenticate callers using OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens and enforce role‑based access control to ensure users only receive data for which they have permission.
QEDS shall respond to each valid query within 500 milliseconds under a load of up to 1000 concurrent queries, measured from request receipt to response transmission.

QEDS shall return HTTP status codes: 200 for success, 400 for invalid parameters, 401 for unauthorized access, and 503 for service unavailability, with appropriate error messages in the response body.
QEDS shall cache results of identical query parameter sets for 30 seconds to improve response performance, and shall invalidate the cache when underlying data changes.

low_level_requirements.requirement: QEDS shall accept polling requests from authorized clients via a REST API endpoint /poll using HTTP POST.
QEDS shall validate each polling request against a defined JSON schema that includes client_id, timestamp, and data_filters fields.
QEDS shall authenticate the client using an API key or OAuth token and reject requests with invalid credentials.
QEDS shall retrieve the most recent environmental data records from the Qualified Environmental Data store based on the request's time window and sensor identifiers.
QEDS shall filter the retrieved data to include only records that match the specified data_filters criteria.
QEDS shall enrich each data record with metadata including source identifier, acquisition timestamp, and quality flag.
QEDS shall format the filtered and enriched data into a JSON response payload that conforms to the Clarus polling response schema.
QEDS shall deliver the response to the client within 500 milliseconds from receipt of the request.
QEDS shall log each polling transaction, capturing request details, response size, and processing time for audit purposes.
QEDS shall return appropriate HTTP status codes and descriptive error messages for malformed requests, authentication failures, or data unavailability.

Revision rules:

- Remove speculative, duplicate, unverifiable, ambiguous, or implementation-specific LLRs.

- Preserve the HLR's intent and modal wording.
